image: low power shows a tumor in the subretinal area.jpg
res:
Microscopic description: The image shows a well-demarcated, nodular lesion with a central area of necrosis. The tumor cells are arranged in sheets and fascicles, with some areas showing a pseudo-palisading pattern around the necrotic core. The nuclei are large, hyperchromatic, and show prominent nucleoli. There is no significant invasion into adjacent tissue.
Key supporting features: Sheets and fascicles; large hyperchromatic nuclei; prominent nucleoli; central necrosis.
Likely interpretation: Squamous cell carcinoma; differential includes sarcoma and lymphoma. Microscopic description: The image displays a lesion with a mixed architecture, predominantly composed of spindle-shaped cells arranged in fascicles and nests. There is a central area of hemorrhage and necrosis. The cells have elongated nuclei with irregular shapes, prominent nucleoli, and scant cytoplasm. There is minimal invasion into the surrounding stroma.
Key supporting features: Spindle-shaped cells; fascicles and nests; irregular
gts:
Pathologic diagnosis: Choroidal melanoma.
Microscopic findings: Low power view shows an intraocular melanoma mass involving the posterior choroid/subretinal region.

res:
Microscopic description: The lesion is a well-demarcated, irregularly shaped area with a mixed architecture of sheets and nests of atypical cells. The cells are predominantly epithelioid in shape, with moderate to abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm. There is a marked nuclear pleomorphism with prominent nucleoli and frequent mitotic figures. The atypical cells show a close relationship with the surrounding normal squamous epithelium, invading its borders.

Key supporting features: Sheets; Nests; Atypical epithelioid cells; Nuclear pleomorphism; Prominent nucleoli; Frequent mitotic figures;1; Close relationship with normal squamous epithelium

Likely interpretation: Squamous cell carcinoma in situ with invasive component; differential includes basal cell carcinoma. (uncertain based on limited information). There is no significant cytoplasmic pigmentation noted.

Key supporting features: Nodular; irregular geometry; epithelioid cells; variable nuclear size; mild to moderate pleomorphism; mitotic figures; no pigmentation.
